## Summary
Defines the reusable pack format, validation rules, deployment lifecycle, and governance contract for digital employees.

## Extracted Preview
> Ruh.ai V1 Employee Pack Specification
> Reusable packaging standard for governed digital employees built on OpenClaw gateway runtimes
> Document type
> Version
> Status

> System contract
> 1.0
> Working draft
> 7 March 2026
> 
> What this document does. It defines the reusable pack format, authoring rules, collaboration contracts, governance model, validation gates, and deployment lifecycle used to create digital employees that can work together across many customers without forking the product.
> 
> Companion documents. Use this specification together with the platform PRD, the V1 System Design & MVP Execution Blueprint, and the V1 API & Event Contract Specification. The pack spec is the bridge between architecture and deployable employees.
> 
> Figure 1. The employee pack is the reusable source artifact; tenant overlays and runtime bundles stay separate.
> Executive snapshot
> A pack is the highest-level reusable product artifact in Ruh.ai. It is the unit that product, solution engineering, and platform engineering can author once and deploy many times. A pack does not represent a single tenant deployment. Instead it packages customer-neutral definitions for employee roles, workflow contracts, artifact schemas, approval logic, connector mappings, and eval suites.
> The central design decision is that packs remain declarative by default and customer-specific values live in overlays. That gives Ruh.ai a way to serve multiple construction companies, software teams, and other future industries without cloning prompts or maintaining one-off agent workspaces for each customer.
